founder.name: Niloy Saha
founder.short_bio: Visionary founder dedicated to empowering Bangladeshi youth.
founder.bio:
Meet Niloy Saha, a passionate public speaker, policy advocate, and development enthusiast from Bangladesh. He is currently pursuing a BA in Philosophy at the University of Rajshahi. With over six years of hands-on experience, Niloy focuses on civic innovation, digital inclusion, and governance through youth-led initiatives that empower communities.

As the Founder and CEO of the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF) and a lead in Youth for Policy (YFP), Niloy has guided diverse teams in projects that build digital skills and promote ethical online engagement. His key efforts include leading the Digital Citizenship and Project Shore O initiatives, where he helped more than 100 young people in Bangladesh gain essential tools for civic tech and digital democracy.

Niloy brings strong skills in project management, monitoring and evaluation, research, communication, grant writing, and team leadership. He has partnered with civil society groups, run workshops, and advocated for inclusive governance. His background in philosophy strengthens his ethical approach to supporting marginalized voices.

Honored with fellowships like Youth for Policy and national awards such as the President Scout Award, Niloy stays committed to advancing democratic values through local and regional work in South Asia. His career goals include leading civic tech programs, applying his expertise in grant-making and advocacy, coordinating peer learning, and contributing to organizations that promote accountability and sustainable digital governance.

coreTeam.name: Md. Reashad Akhter
coreTeam.bio: Meet Md. Reashad Akhter, a skilled brand manager and marketing professional with expertise in international marketing, communications, and event management from Bangladesh. He holds an MBA in Marketing from Bangladesh University of Professionals and a BBA in Marketing with a minor in Human Resource Management from the same institution. Currently serving as Brand Manager for International Marketing at SQUARE Food & Beverage Ltd. since June 2022, he spearheads product development, event planning, data analysis, audits, promotions, trademark processes, and packaging management for global markets. Previously, as Advertising and Communication Secretary at Star T Vision and Event from March 2018 to April 2020, he developed campaigns, created promotional materials, coordinated teams, and managed social media to enhance event visibility. Proficient in maintaining effective communications, promotional activities, advertising, and cultural affairs, Reashad aims to drive brand growth and strategic marketing initiatives in the food and beverage sector.

coreTeam.name: Rafia Jebin
coreTeam.bio: Jebin Rafia is a motivated social development enthusiast with an academic background in social sciences. She completed her Bachelor of Social Science (BSS) from Begum Badrunnessa Government Girls' College in 2023 and previously studied at Rajbari Government College and Rajbari Government Girls School. She is committed to working in the development sector, with a particular interest in community empowerment, participatory development, and social justice initiatives. currently serves as the Vice President of the Rajbari Socio-Cultural Forum, where she engages in community-focused initiatives and organizational activities. Her professional interests include climate justice, community empowerment, and inclusive development. She has developed key competencies in academic writing, policy analysis, teamwork, adaptability, and social media management, which support her ability to contribute effectively to development-oriented programs and initiatives.

coreTeam.name: Redwan Ahmed
coreTeam.bio: Meet Redwan Ahmed, a motivated graduate and development enthusiast from Rajshahi, Bangladesh. He holds a BA in Philosophy from the University of Rajshahi. Since December 2024, he has served as a business intern at Logistica, applying his skills to support operations. In Project Sware-O with Team Nirvana, he helped enhance digital literacy for youth in Rajshahi to build a smarter Bangladesh. His achievements include first runner-up in Policy Hackathon 2024, participation in Youth Policy Summit 2024, Policy CampX, inter-year debate competition, and a workshop on youth engagement and democracy by Manusher Jonno Foundation. As general secretary of Rajshahi University Philosophy Students' Alliance, treasurer for Youth for Policy's Rajshahi District Committee, director of events at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum, lead in administration and procurement for Team Nirvana, media secretary at Rajshahi University Photographic Club, and former associate in groups like Rajshahi University Model United Nations Association and SILSWA, he fosters leadership, research, and community activities. Skilled in policy analysis, document management, digital literacy course management, education, and project management, Redwan aims to contribute to sustainable growth and social impact through inclusive and equitable development solutions.

coreTeam.name: Aurit Ahsan Ohi
coreTeam.bio: Aurit Ahsan is a law graduate from the University of Rajshahi with a strong academic foundation and an active engagement in youth leadership, policy discourse, and human rights advocacy. With a background in legal studies and public engagement, he has developed a reputation as a motivated and forward-thinking individual committed to promoting liberty, social justice, and meaningful social change. He has gained practical experience through an internship at the Office of the Attorney-General for Bangladesh under the AGO-UNDP Internship Program and serves as the Local Coordinator (South Asia) for Students for Liberty, where he contributes to regional youth initiatives and leadership development. Additionally, he has been involved in international and regional programs such as the CAYA Advocacy Fellowship and the UMSAILS-UAP Spring School on Forced Displacement, reflecting his growing engagement with global legal and policy discussions. Aurit has also demonstrated leadership within the University of Rajshahi community as the Senior Vice-President of the Rajshahi University Debating Organization (RUDO) and previously served as Head of Communication for an international conference on Human Rights and Social Justice. His achievements include recognition at the NILS-LEB National Model Legislative Assembly and participation in the Youth Policy Summit 2024. Alongside his legal interests, he possesses skills in digital tools, graphic design, and event management, and remains actively engaged in debating, youth activism, and advocacy initiatives.

coreTeam.name: Sadia Nowshin Sharin
coreTeam.bio:
Nowshin Sharin is a young professional dedicated to advancing cultural dialogue and community development. As the Assistant Director of External Affairs at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), she actively works to strengthen partnerships, promote Bangladeshi heritage, and engage youth in creative, socially impactful initiatives.

A graduate of Daffodil International University, Nowshin brings a strong background in communication, media, and organizational management. Alongside her role at BSCF, she serves as a staff reporter at Centrist Nation TV, where she covers stories on social issues, culture, and youth perspectives. She also works remotely as an administrative assistant for a U.S.-based Home-Health Aid Agency, gaining valuable experience in global coordination and professional management.

Through her diverse roles, Nowshin blends media insight, cultural advocacy, and administrative expertise to contribute meaningfully to BSCF’s mission of empowering youth, fostering social awareness, and building bridges between Bangladesh and the global community.

coreTeam.name: Arabinda Banerjee Anik
coreTeam.bio: Arabinda Banerjee is a Physics undergraduate at University of Rajshahi, currently pursuing his Bachelor of Science degree with a strong interest in leadership, organizational management, and strategic program execution. Alongside his academic journey, he has actively engaged in various leadership and operational roles, including serving as the Director (Branding & Promotions) at the Rajshahi University Business Club, where he coordinates administrative activities and supports the execution of large-scale programs. He is also the District Lead for Youth For Policy in Rajshahi, overseeing volunteer coordination and stakeholder engagement across district initiatives. As a Founding Member and Assistant Director of Finance at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), he contributes to budgeting, sponsorship management, and financial planning for organizational programs and community campaigns. Arabinda has also worked as a Trainer Management Associate at Kore Search and currently serves as a Campus Ambassador for SheSTEM, supporting recruitment and partnership coordination. Recognized for his involvement in competitions and leadership activities, he has been a finalist and semifinalist in several national case competitions and debates. With skills in financial coordination, stakeholder engagement, and digital tools such as Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint, Canva, and Adobe Premiere Pro, he aims to develop a career that combines analytical thinking, leadership, and impactful organizational work.

coreTeam.name: Zarin Roshni
coreTeam.bio: Zarin Roshni is a Philosophy student at the University of Rajshahi with a growing interest in leadership, communication, and project management. She aims to apply her organizational and leadership abilities in collaborative environments where she can contribute to team success and community-focused initiatives. She currently serves as the Assistant Director of the Communication Department at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), a non-profit organization dedicated to promoting science, culture, and social awareness among youth. Through this role, she contributes to communication initiatives and student engagement activities. She has also worked as a journalist for the children’s magazine Duronto Shishu-Kishor Potrika, where she contributed to creative and youth-oriented publications. Additionally, she has experience as a poetry instructor and early childhood educator at Alo Academy of Art, where she supported young learners through creative and play-based educational activities. Academically, she is pursuing a BA in Philosophy at the University of Rajshahi, after completing her HSC and SSC with excellent academic results from institutions in Khulna. Her skills include communication, team management, branding and promotion, leadership, and presentation. Alongside her academic and professional engagements, she actively participates in social and educational initiatives, including the BSCF Astronomy Camp, reflecting her enthusiasm for learning, teamwork, and community involvement.

coreTeam.name: Safiat Rahaman Sifat
coreTeam.bio: Safiat Rahaman Sifat is a communications and policy-oriented youth leader with extensive experience in grassroots initiatives, program development, and youth engagement. Known for his strong leadership in youth-led organizations and policy platforms, he has worked across NGOs, think tanks, and civic initiatives to promote community participation, policy dialogue, and youth empowerment. He is the Co-Founder and Chief of Grassroots at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he leads volunteer mobilization, partnership development, and community outreach programs. In addition, he serves as a Policy Associate at the Youth Policy Forum (YPF), contributing to national policy discussions, youth dialogues, and behavioral economics research initiatives. He has also been involved with the Youth Engagement in Democracy (YED) Project under the Rajbari Socio-Cultural Club, where he led district-level workshops, regional debate summits, and youth engagement programs. Previously, he worked with the Youth School for Social Entrepreneurship (YSSE), where he progressed from an intern to a senior associate in communications, managing large intern cohorts and leading high-impact projects such as national case competitions and strategic communication campaigns. He also contributes as a Content Developer and Youth Mentor for initiatives under Grameenphone Academy and LCOY Bangladesh, supporting climate dialogue and digital learning programs for young leaders. Academically, he completed his SSC and HSC with outstanding results in the science stream and has received several recognitions for leadership and participation in national and international initiatives. His experience spans youth policy advocacy, program management, communication strategy, and community-driven development, reflecting his commitment to empowering young people and creating meaningful social impact.

coreTeam.name: Avik Arefin
coreTeam.bio:
Avik Arefin is a Mechatronics Engineer from Rajshahi University of Engineering and Technology (RUET), recognized for his ability to integrate complex hardware systems with scalable software solutions. His technical portfolio is defined by international success, most notably as a lead member of Team Cortex Robotics. Under his technical contribution, the team secured 1st Place in Bangladesh, and 4th Place Worldwide for the 2025 JAXA-NASA Kibo Robot Challenge. His expertise also extends to machine learning and global tech advocacy, evidenced by his selection as a finalist in Huawei’s Seeds for the Future and his Top 10 finish at the Tech4Good competition in China.
In the commercial sector, Avik bridges the gap between engineering theory and consumer-ready products. He currently spearheads the development of the Armor Gold Secure Locker App for the US-based Aethes Corporation. His versatility as a developer is further demonstrated by the successful launch of diverse web platforms, including logistica4u.com and bscf.dev. Under his belt he experience includes, including industrial training at Forbes Marshall and a certification in Supervised Machine Learning from Stanford/DeepLearning AI.

Beyond his technical roles, he is serving as an Assistant Director at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum. Driven by a goal to solve real-world problems, Avik is focused on developing the next generation of consumer-centric technology that addresses both local and global challenges.

coreTeam.name: Ahnaf Tahsin Monmoy
coreTeam.bio: Ahnaf Tahsin Monmoy is an undergraduate student at the University of Rajshahi pursuing a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Accounting and Information Systems. He is known for his analytical mindset and strong interest in integrating technology with financial and accounting practices to support data-driven decision-making and efficient financial management.Alongside his academic studies, he has demonstrated entrepreneurial initiative as the Founder and CEO of Brogotflix, a streaming service platform, and as the Co-Founder of Mobile Guru, a business operating at Jamuna Future Park. Through these roles, he has gained practical experience in business management, digital media, and financial operations.  Ahnaf is also actively engaged in extracurricular and professional communities. He serves as an Associate Member of the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF) and the Rajshahi University Professional Development Club, where he participates in leadership and professional development activities. His technical and professional skills include financial management, graphic design, digital media management, and proficiency in Microsoft Office tools.Overall, he aspires to build a career at the intersection of finance, technology, and business innovation, leveraging analytical thinking and technological tools to contribute to the evolving field of accounting and financial systems.

coreTeam.name: Abdullah Al Hossain Sami
coreTeam.bio: Abdullah Al Hossain Sami is a student of the University of Rajshahi currently pursuing a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Accounting and Information Systems. He is an enthusiastic and creative individual with a strong interest in music, cultural activities, leadership, and student engagement. Through his academic and extracurricular involvement, he has developed skills in communication, teamwork, and program coordination. He currently serves as the Assistant Director (Cultural) at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he contributes to planning and executing cultural programs at institutional and national levels. In this role, he supports team coordination, organizes workshops and performances, and promotes creative participation among students. Earlier, he worked as a Project Management Officer at Cultural Aethetes, where he helped organize cultural and educational programs aimed at enhancing student creativity and co-curricular engagement. Sami has also taken leadership roles in several projects and events. He served as the Convener of the Coordination Committee for the BSCF 2nd Astronomy Camp 2025, overseeing planning, logistics, and cross-team communication. Additionally, he worked as the Project In-Charge for the SkinCafe Youth Carnival – Rajshahi Chapter, successfully coordinating the event across eight districts of the Rajshahi Division. Alongside these roles, he gained field experience as a Field Officer with ICAN-ICARE (PalNetwork) Bangladesh, where he conducted field assessments and data collection related to foundational learning initiatives. His technical and creative skills include Microsoft Office, Tally ERP basics, poster and visual design, public speaking, music composition, photography, and content creation. Overall, he demonstrates a strong commitment to cultural development, youth engagement, and leadership within student communities.

coreTeam.name: kazi Abdullah Al Sahil
coreTeam.bio: Sahil is an undergraduate student at University of Rajshahi pursuing a degree in Materials Science and Engineering. He is known for his analytical mindset and strong interest in combining scientific knowledge with leadership and organizational development. Alongside his academic studies, he actively contributes to student and youth initiatives. He serves as the Assistant Director of Events & Administration at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he supports event planning, administrative coordination, and the successful execution of organizational programs. He also works as a Public Relations Officer at Youth For Policy, engaging in communication, outreach, and stakeholder engagement to promote youth participation in policy-related discussions.Additionally, he completed an internship under the SheSTEM Leadership Programme, where he gained experience in leadership development and professional collaboration. With a strong foundation in analytical thinking, communication, and teamwork, he aspires to build a career that integrates scientific innovation with impactful leadership and community engagement.

coreTeam.name: Talha Zubayer
coreTeam.bio: Talha Zubayer is an aspiring engineer and undergraduate student in Industrial and Production Engineering at Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology. Known for his creativity and leadership, he actively combines technical learning with digital media, design, and organizational initiatives. Alongside his academic journey, he serves as the Assistant Director (Creative) at Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he contributes to creative direction, visual communication, and program execution for organizational activities. He has also worked as a Creative Executive for BSCF Digital, supporting digital content development and branding initiatives. Talha played an important role in organizing major programs such as the First Divisional BSCF Astronomy Camp 2024, where he served as Director of Arts, and the Second National BSCF Astronomy Camp 2025, where he contributed as a mentor. In addition to his organizational involvement, he works as a Graphic and Web Designer for the clothing brand Fenzy, applying his skills in graphic design, digital marketing, video editing, and content creation. With a strong academic background and a passion for creativity and leadership, Talha aims to build a career that integrates engineering innovation with digital media and impactful organizational work.

coreTeam.name: S.M. Labib
coreTeam.bio: Labib Ahmed is an emerging Graphic Design and Multimedia professional currently pursuing a Bachelor of Honours in Graphic Design & Multimedia at Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology. He is actively involved in the creative industry and currently serves as the Assistant Creative Director at BSCF in Dhaka, Bangladesh. In this role, he contributes to branding initiatives, social media campaigns, and the development of high-quality visual content while collaborating with cross-functional teams and mentoring junior designers. He possesses strong expertise in digital design tools and multimedia production, including Adobe Photoshop, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe After Effects, and Adobe Premiere Pro. His professional interests focus on logo design, branding, social media visuals, video editing, typography, layout design, and motion graphics. Known for his creativity and passion for visual storytelling, he aims to deliver impactful digital media solutions that effectively communicate ideas and strengthen brand identity.

coreTeam.name: Nafis Iqbal Niloy
coreTeam.bio: Nafis Iqbal Niloy is a creative media enthusiast with a strong interest in photography, cinematography, and visual storytelling. Currently pursuing a Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) in Accounting, he combines academic learning with practical experience in creative media production and event coordination. He serves as the Creative Director at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where he leads and supervises creative media projects, including promotional videos, branding materials, and visual campaigns. Through this role, he contributes to developing engaging content and managing creative teams for various organizational initiatives. In addition, he is the Founder and Creative Lead of Clicks of Life, a visual media platform specializing in photography and cinematography. Through this initiative, he has gained hands-on experience in directing, media production, and end-to-end photo and video editing projects. His technical expertise includes tools such as Lightroom and CapCut, along with strong skills in professional photography, cinematography, and creative direction.Alongside his technical abilities, Niloy demonstrates strong communication, leadership, and event management skills. His contributions have also been recognized through awards such as the “Best Mentor in Poster Presentation” at BSCF in November 2025. Overall, he continues to develop as a creative professional focused on impactful visual communication and media production.

coreTeam.name: Mst Anika Akter
coreTeam.bio: Meet Anika Akter, a motivated and detail-oriented professional with experience in customer service, event coordination, and community engagement. She currently serves as Assistant Director (Procurement) at the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), where she supports program and event logistics, manages vendor coordination, and ensures timely procurement of materials for diverse social and educational initiatives. She has played a key role in organizing women-centric and child education programs that foster empowerment and learning. Previously, she worked as an Executive at Conscious Creative Home and Color Expression, where she coordinated client communications, vendor relations, budgeting, and event planning for cultural programs, concerts, and television projects. She also has hands-on experience in hospitality as a waitress at Masala Inn, providing excellent guest service and maintaining high operational standards. She holds both a Bachelor’s and Master’s degree in Bengali Literature from the National University of Bangladesh. Alongside her administrative and coordination expertise, she is skilled in financial handling, digital content support, barista training, and preparing traditional South Asian dishes. Fluent in Bengali, English, and conversational Hindi, Anika embodies professionalism, cultural awareness, and a passion for community-driven impact.

coreTeam.name: Fatema Tuz Jannat Noor
coreTeam.bio: Most. Fatema Tuz Jannat Noor is a final-year undergraduate student of Mass Communication and Journalism at Bangladesh University of Professionals (BUP) with a strong academic focus on research, policy analysis, and strategic communication. She has developed experience in interdisciplinary research and evidence-based reporting, demonstrating a keen interest in advocacy, climate discourse, and development policy.She currently serves as a Youth Fellow at the International Centre for Climate Change and Development (ICCCAD), where she participates in policy discussions and youth-led initiatives related to climate justice, development, and social inclusion. In addition, she works as a Research Associate at Lal Sabuj Society and previously contributed as a Research Assistant at Bangladesh University of Professionals, supporting faculty-led research projects and co-authoring multiple academic papers presented at international conferences. She also served as the Lead Analyst and Report Writer for the Child Situation Report 2025, producing data-driven policy recommendations.Beyond research roles, Noor has demonstrated strong communication and coordination skills through her involvement as the Public Relations and Administration Head for the Lunar Satellite Project (NASA GLEE Mission under the a2i Programme), where she led communication strategies and stakeholder engagement. Her skill set includes qualitative and quantitative research methods, report writing, media analysis, data analysis using Excel and SPSS, and bilingual communication in Bangla and English.Through conference presentations, workshops, and youth policy dialogues, she has actively contributed to discussions on climate change, media representation, social inclusion, and development challenges. Overall, she is recognized as a research-oriented and policy-focused young professional committed to using communication and evidence-based research to address social and environmental issues.

coreTeam.name: Jariat Maisha
coreTeam.bio: Jariat Maisha is a Business Administration student at the University of Rajshahi, currently pursuing a Bachelor of Business Administration with an expected graduation in 2026. She is known for being responsible, organized, and eager to gain practical experience while continuously developing her academic knowledge and professional skills.She completed her Higher Secondary Certificate from Gazipur Cantonment College under the Dhaka Board in the Business Studies group and her Secondary School Certificate from Bari High School. Through her academic journey, she has shown interest in building a strong foundation in business and management. In addition to her studies, Maisha has developed skills in photography, videography, and presentation design. She also gained practical volunteer experience through a blood donation campaign in Rajshahi in February 2024, where she assisted in organizing a blood donation drive involving more than 50 donors. During this initiative, she coordinated with volunteers from the Red Crescent and helped maintain organizational records, strengthening her communication and teamwork abilities.Overall, Jariat Maisha aims to build a career in a challenging professional environment where she can apply her knowledge, enhance her skills, and contribute positively to organizational growth while gaining meaningful real-world experience.

coreTeam.name: Ramisha Mohsin
coreTeam.bio: Ramisha Mohsin is an enthusiastic architecture student at Rajshahi University of Engineering & Technology (RUET), where she has been pursuing her Bachelor of Architecture since 2022. Known for her problem-solving mindset and leadership abilities, she combines strong technical skills in tools like AutoCAD, Archicad, and Adobe Photoshop with a passion for sustainable design and community engagement. She has actively participated in competitive design platforms, including the IAB ArchJAM 2025, and was named Champion of the Heritage Hiking 2026 program at the Varendra Research Museum. Alongside her academic pursuits, Ramisha has built leadership experience as a STEM Leadership Intern and Creative Executive for the Bangladesh Socio-Cultural Forum (BSCF), while also volunteering for the RUET Architecture Department Seminar Library. Deeply motivated to use design as a tool for inclusivity—particularly to support special children and elderly people—she aspires to become a responsible architect who delivers innovative spaces that positively impact society.

coreTeam.name: Sadiya Somayea
coreTeam.bio: Sadiya Somayea is a Philosophy student at the University of Rajshahi, currently pursuing a Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) degree with an expected graduation in 2027. Known for being a motivated and responsible individual, she is focused on developing strong analytical, critical thinking, and academic research skills through her studies. She completed her Higher Secondary Certificate from Rajshahi Govt. Women's College under the Rajshahi Board in the Humanities group and her Secondary School Certificate from Katila Shabuj Shangha Adarsha M.L. High School. In addition to her academic pursuits, Sadiya has developed a versatile skillset that includes creative thinking, basic computer literacy in MS Word and PowerPoint, photography, videography, and strong communication, teamwork, and leadership abilities. She has also gained practical collaborative experience by actively engaging in socio-cultural and youth programs, where she has supported the organization of events, managed program activities, and worked comfortably across multiple languages, including Bangla, English, and conversational Hindi. Overall, she seeks an opportunity to utilize her diverse skills and knowledge to contribute positively to an organization while gaining valuable professional experience.
